After the transcendent experience of last year's I.T. presents T4T LUV NRG, and hot on the heels of two Samhains (XXIII & XXIV), wešre bringing back the LUV NRG Soundsystem to launch the eternal beat ą the Motherbeat ą for the weekend, as the second of 6 events IT will be presenting at the Tangent from May 21 til May 25. This night will celebrate a wider view of T4T with both rooms curated to that vision.

Coming all the way from New Hampshire, T4T LUV NRGšs mobile sound rig is a monument of intention, conceived by Eris & Octa, and operated by Faited of Oaklandšs Envelope Soundsystem. The LUV NRG Soundsystem will be set up in the Ballroom and helmed by the beloved duo from New York and Philadelphia č Boyz II Them (Russell E.L. Butler & ADAB), with a special extended closing set from Octo Octa & Eris Drew.

The Gallery is the bass zone at this yearšs T4T. Renowned international producer and T4T recording artist Introspekt will be closing the room with her profound psychedelic and sensual bass explorations. Sister Zo, also performing, is renowned for powerfully fusing all the forms of bass music with elements of techno, Bristol rhythms, and drum and bass. Opening the room will be Detroitšs own Amino, with her deeply thoughtful and powerful sound. Truly a night not to be missed.

Full venue transformation by Amber Gillen & the Infinite Dimensions Crew. Full audiophile sound by Jim Gibbons AVS. LUV NRG Soundsystem presented by T4T LUV NRG and operated by Faited. At the ever evolving Tangent Gallery.

column2: 100% LIVE Techno ą ALL HARDWARE SETS
column3:
Experience the raw, unfiltered core of techno at the 7th installment of 100% LIVE, Detroitšs most unique event during Movement Festival Weekend. Shunning curated playlists for spontaneous creation, masters of the craft utilize synthesizers and drum machines to forge a dynamic, unpredictable sonic journey in real time.

Every pulse is delivered through a premium VOID sound system, ensuring the intense vibrations of hardware-driven music resonate through your core. Join us to celebrate this untamed legacy where the music is born and experienced in a single, fleeting instant.

After two sold-out years, we are teaming up with DVS1 again, bringing Wall of Sound back to Detroit for a third edition on Saturday, May 23rd

Room 1 featuring DVS1, Claudio PRC, Juana, R┐dhīd & Traversable Wormhole
Room 2 featuring Justin Aulis Long & Traxx

This project has always been about creating the right conditions for the music to exist as itšs meant to be heard. A system built for depth and pressure, a room designed around sound, lighting carefully curated to enhance the experience, and a crowd willing to be fully present with it.

If youšve been before, you already understand.
If you havenšt, this is your moment.

PLEASE WEAR HEARING PROTECTION.

NO RE-ENTRY

column3:
During Movement Festival Weekend, Texture returns to Marble Bar to celebrate its 10-year anniversary with a two-day sonic journey in psychedelic club sounds; where dub mutates into leftfield electronics, and electro shakes hands with bass. A continuum built for deep listening and full-body presence.

Over the past decade, what began as an experiment in personal expression has evolved into a platform for exploration.

How can we combine sound design, temporary spatial architecture, and light design recalibrate the body?
How can a room become an instrument?
How can creating conditions with no defined outcomes leave space for new potentials?

Detroit-based light artist Patrick Ethen is back to reshape Marble Bar into an environment of controlled disorientation.

Ten years in, the core remains the same. Stay curious.
Event admin
JacobPark

column3: Bowel Movement is the only solid Movement

On Saturday, May 23, Alternative School presents SUBSONIC at Northern Lights Lounge, headlined by the architect of Acid House, the legendary DJ Pierre. This night is a deep dive into the raw soul of the genre and a sound that remains a significant, defining moment in electronic music history.

The foundation of this sound traces back to mid 1980s Chicago and the Roland TB 303. Pierre and the group Phuturečjoined by Earl Spanky Smith Jr. and Herbert Herb J Jacksončfamously reimagined the machine. They took a bass synthesizer originally designed to emulate a real bass guitar and pushed it past its intended limits. By twisting the resonance and cutoff knobs until the hardware literally screamed, they birthed the liquid, psychedelic squelch of Acid Tracks. That curiosity proved that by seeing a machine in a different way, you could create an entirely new reality for the dancefloor.

DJ Pierre brings his Wild Pitch and Afro Acid philosophy to the booth, joined by a heavy hitting roster: Duck Trash, Father Dukes, Garrison XR, MGUN, Posthuman, Ryan Spencer, Sneaker, and a live set from Wetdogg.

SUBSONIC brings these forces together at Northern Lights Lounge for a night rooted in the true spirit of the craft.

column2: Detroit Techno Militia x Techno Snob
column3:
Detroit Techno Militia and Techno Snob link up for a night rooted in the city that started it all. No hype cycles, no nostalgia grabs...just the sound of Detroit techno played by the people who live it, build it, and keep it moving forward.

This isnšt about exclusivity. Itšs about community, culture, and the shared language of drum machines, and late-night dance floors. From small basements to global clubs, Detroit has always influenced the world without asking for permission. This night honors that tradition.

Expect tightly curated sets from selectors and artists who know how to read a room, stretch a groove, and tell a story without saying a word. Deep cuts, future classics, unreleased heat, hardware workouts╔ the kind of thing that reminds you why Detroit still matters in 2026 and beyond.

Whether youšre a lifer or stepping into the scene for the first time, youšre welcome here. Come dressed to sweat.

No gimmicks. No dress code. No VIP ropes. Just music, connection, and a room full of people who understand why techno still hits hardest when itšs close to the source.

Detroit Techno Militia _ Techno Snob Detroit.
All night. All techno.

Start your morning right with some tech house, uk garage, and high energy dance music. Sevag H and FrankJavCee go B2B for the first time, with ARCS and Sam Haux bringing tech house and techno sounds to kick off the party.

May 23rd from 2pm to 2am
Location via (313) 513-RAVE on the day

---

To identify the patterns, links, dissonances; each apparatus possesses its own little music, which must be put slightly out of tune, incidentally distorted, pushed to decay, to destruction, to become unhinged. Those who flow into the apparatus donšt notice the music, their steps stick too close to the rhythm to hear it distinctly. For the latter, another temporality is needed, a specific rhythmicity, so that, although we enter the apparatus, we remain attentive to the prevailing norm. That is what the thief, the criminal learns: to unsync internal and external tempos, to split, to layer onešs conscience, being at once mobile and static, on the lookout and deceptively static... To turn the imposed schizophrenia of self-control into an offensive conspiratorial instrument.

---

Ticker tiers are demographic based. Tranny for all trans people, SE Michigan for "locals", Import for all others. Residency may be verified at the door.

Please carpool or ride share to the event. Parking is highly limited. Do not park on the street or we will not let you in.

No phones. No cameras. No trackers. Anywhere in the party. Turn your phone off and keep it in your pocket. Be present, don't be vain, and keep our community safe.

Help us reduce our footprint. Bring your own reusable cup. Clean up after yourself. Be responsible.

MOODS BBQ (Movement Weekend) ŌĆö Trail Conditions + Weather Advisory
ATTN: HIKERS, NEIGHBORS, DAY TRIPPERS, AND SEASONAL VISITORS:

THIS IS A JOINT BULLETIN FROM THE MOODS TRAIL SERVICE (MTS) AND THE NATIONAL OCEANIC & MOODSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION (NOMA).

A persistent high-pressure system of dancing is expected to move into Detroit during Movement weekend, bringing sustained joy, laughter, and reunion hugs.

PREPAREDNESS IS KEY
If you plan on enjoying the new Moods Trail on May 22, 2026ŌĆöa scenic loop through sweat, shade, and soft-serious joy that appears briefly each year only to disappear back into legend:

Please have your valid entry documents ready for inspection at the trailhead. Passports, Real IDs, and Permission Slips to Groove are all accepted. As a gentle reminder: bad attitudes and egos must be secured in a bear- or twink-proof container before crossing into protected land. If you are unable or unwilling to perform basic trail functionsŌĆöincluding dancing, hydrating, and sharingŌĆöplease notify the nearest Moods Ranger. WeŌĆÖll gladly reroute you to a quieter outlook, a different trailhead, or a clout cookout in the region.

Moods Rangers also recommend the following items for your hike:

Comfy hiking clothes to wear
Water bottles & drinks to share
Small personal bags
Lawn chairs
Ear plugs
Lawn Blankets

FORECAST & CONDITIONS
Time window: 2p-8p
Heat index: hydration advisory expected; i.e. ŌĆ£you should probably drink waterŌĆØ
Visibility: sunglasses recommended
Terrain: open-air among local flora and fauna
Wildlife advisory: do not feed the drama (it becomes dependent)
Bear advisory: please report any bear sightings to Moods Ranger Daniel immediately

As the system settles, the trail will gradually guide all travelers toward the final landmark:

THE CLEARING (Summit Reward)
At the end of your route, youŌĆÖll arrive at a brand new location where [REDACTED] will be serving food to those who complete the hike. Expect smoke, salt, a plate in hand, and the kind of satisfaction that makes you stay longer than planned.

The following Moods Rangers will be on-site to provide your summit with sound:

Niyah West b2b Mira Mira
John Collins b2b Michael S.
Santiago Salazar b2b Sugar

If you attempt to visit past trail locations, you will be met with only the sounds of local birds and fauna.

For more info, consult the visitor guide below or ask a Moods Ranger stationed along the way.

Thank you for visiting. We hope you enjoy your time on the Moods Trail.

column2: Official Movement Opening Party // Speedy J // Drumcell // DJ Hyperactive
column3:
When it comes to raw, uncompromising techno, few names carry the weight of Speedy J. A true pioneer of the genre, the Dutch producer and DJ has been shaping underground electronic music since the early š90s, earning a reputation for powerful, immersive sets that blur the line between classic Detroit influence and cutting-edge sound design.

On Movement Electronic Music Festival weekend, Speedy J brings his signature intensity to the Movement Official Opening Party at Lincoln Factory 2026 on May 22nd. Expect a rare opportunity to experience one of technošs most respected innovators as he sets the tone for the weekend in Detroit.

Endzeit is back in Detroit for a second year, returning to the creative home that shaped our project since its inception. In Detroit, techno emerged amidst industrial decline and automation as a portal toward possible futures: music that can be mechanical, physical, and emotional, using machine rhythms to induce hypnosis and transcendence. That legacy draws us back each year, a pilgrimage for those in communion with sound.

On May 22, Endzeit brings returning favorites, first-time guests, and Detroit debuts to Detroit institution Marble Bar, home to an exceptional soundsystem and a patio perfect for dancing past dawn.

Art Of Techno (US) is a grassroots movement dedicated to bringing cutting-edge music and culture to the forefront. Showcasing top-tier local, regional, and international talent, each event delivers a powerful blend of driving techno and forward-thinking underground music.

While the main room focuses on techno, a second room often features other genres such as house and drum & bass, keeping the classic multi-room underground vibe alive.

With a focus on community, creativity, and the raw energy of the underground, Art Of Techno creates an immersive experience where artists and dancers come together to celebrate the spirit of electronic music.

Detroit Techno 5K is a one-of-a-kind running experience that fuses the city's legendary electronic music culture with community fitness. Set against Detroit's iconic backdrop, runners move through the course along the Detroit Riverfront, bringing together music lovers and athletes in a shared celebration of movement and culture. Ending in a finish line DJ party with Jack Beats. Whether you're a seasoned runner or lacing up for the first time, this event is built for everyone who wants to experience Detroit differently. More than a race, it's a movement. Join us and run to the rhythm of the city that invented techno.
